acceptance_criteria_provenance: The r>=0.70 threshold is inherited from the known-gene-benchmark skill's benchmark_threshold=0.7 (fixed in Phase A, before this validation was run). The driver_sign_concordance>=0.80 and all-drivers-program-down bars are conventional sign-consistency checks, not tuned to these data. Observed margins (r=0.93 vs 0.70; concordance 1.0 vs 0.80) are wide. Note: this criteria dict was serialized in the same cell that computed the observed metrics, so the artifact does not itself prove temporal pre-specification of the 0.80 bar — it is documented here as a convention, not claimed as blinded.
notes: Known-gene coefficient recovery is the acceptance test (analog of the AUROC benchmark). Global r across all 3830 genes is low (0.23) by construction - most genes have near-zero effect and the two estimators differ (authors' regularized regression coefficient vs our signature projection); agreement rises monotonically with effect size. RASA2 is the single sign-discordant known gene: our score +0.203 (mid-range brake, comparable to SOCS3/TNFAIP3) vs authors' coef -0.021 (weak driver-end). Separately, the nominated anti-tumor lead RSBN1L (not a benchmark gene) has author_coef=-0.022 (z=-39, more negative than 94% of genes), DISCORDANT with our brake call (+0.554) - disclosed in the report as the primary caution on that lead.

rsbn1l_author_coef_conflict.interpretation: authors' coefficient places RSBN1L at driver/program-down end, opposite our brake call
